description: Canonical CAPS topic map for Grade 4 Mathematics — Terms 1 & 2 (MVP launch scope). Each entry defines the CAPS reference, topic, subtopics, skills, prerequisites, and item generation targets used by the IRT engine and the item generator.
subject: Mathematics

launch_scope.rationale: Grade 4 is the foundation year of the Intermediate Phase. Term 1 covers the three most distinct, prerequisite-linked topic clusters (Whole Numbers, Common Fractions, 2D Shapes) that make an ideal IRT pilot with clear right/wrong answers.

subtopics.subtopic: Count, order and compare whole numbers
learning_outcomes: Count forwards and backwards in 10s, 100s, and 1000s to at least 10 000
Order and compare whole numbers up to at least 9 999
Represent odd and even numbers to at least 1 000
sa_context_examples: Number of cattle in a Limpopo farm
Population of a South African township
Distance between Johannesburg and Durban in kilometres

learning_outcomes: Recognise the place value of digits in whole numbers up to at least 9 999
Decompose 4-digit numbers into thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones
Build numbers from their place value components
sa_context_examples: Price of a school uniform in rands
Number of books in a school library
Seating capacity of a stadium

learning_outcomes: Round off whole numbers to the nearest 10
Round off whole numbers to the nearest 100
Use rounding to estimate answers to calculations
sa_context_examples: Rounding taxi fares to nearest R10
Estimating number of learners in a school

subtopics.subtopic: Number sentences and properties of operations
learning_outcomes: Write number sentences for word problems involving addition and multiplication
Identify the commutative and associative properties of addition and multiplication
sa_context_examples: Sharing sweets equally among friends
Adding up scores in a sports game

learning_outcomes: Name and write fractions including halves, thirds, quarters, fifths, sixths, sevenths, and eighths
Recognise fractions in diagrammatic form
Identify numerator and denominator
sa_context_examples: Sharing a magwinya (fat cake) equally
Cutting a pap portion into equal parts
Dividing a braai between families

learning_outcomes: Compare and order common fractions with the same denominator
Understand that larger denominator means smaller unit fraction (when numerator=1)
Place fractions on a number line
sa_context_examples: Comparing portions of boerewors
Water levels in containers

learning_outcomes: Identify equivalent fractions using diagrams
Find equivalent fractions by multiplying or dividing numerator and denominator by the same number
Recognise that 2/4 = 1/2
sa_context_examples: Splitting a rand coin into equal smaller values

learning_outcomes: Calculate a fraction of a whole number, e.g. 1/4 of 20
Solve word problems involving fractions of quantities
sa_context_examples: 1/4 of a bag of mielie meal
Fraction of learners present at school

learning_outcomes: Name and describe circles, triangles, squares, rectangles, and other quadrilaterals
Describe shapes by their number of sides, angles, and equal sides
Distinguish between regular and irregular shapes
sa_context_examples: Shape of a rondavel (circular hut)
Traditional Ndebele geometric patterns
Shape of a stop sign on South African roads

learning_outcomes: Identify and draw lines of symmetry in 2D shapes
Determine whether a shape is symmetrical
Count the number of lines of symmetry in regular shapes
sa_context_examples: Symmetry in the South African flag
Butterfly wings found in the Kruger National Park

learning_outcomes: Investigate tessellations with regular shapes
Identify which shapes tessellate and why
Create a simple tessellation pattern
sa_context_examples: Tiling patterns on South African pavements
Traditional weaving patterns in baskets

irt_calibration_note: Initial b-parameters are set from difficulty_band at generation time. True IRT calibration (MLE/MAP estimation) runs after the first 100 real diagnostic sessions. Discrimination a=1.0 and guessing c=0.25 are pre-calibration defaults for all 4-option MCQ items.
